content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a function that classifies a person's age into different categories using type annotations. The function should take an integer age as input and return a string representing the age category.

The age categories are as follows:


"Child" for ages 0-12
"Teenager" for ages 13-19
"Adult" for ages 20-64
"Senior" for ages 65 and above
Your task is to implement the `classify_age` function using type annotations to specify the input and output types. The function should take an integer as input and return a string.

Example:
Input: 25
Output: Adult
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer age (0 ≤ age ≤ 150).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the age category: "Child", "Teenager", "Adult" or "Senior".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 10 falls in 0‑12 → Child; 16 falls in 13‑19 → Teenager; 70 ≥ 65 → Senior.
Age 0 is within the Child range.

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Child
Teenager
Teenager
Adult
Adult
Senior

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each age, use a chain of if‑else statements to check the interval it belongs to: 0‑12 → Child, 13‑19 → Teenager, 20‑64 → Adult, otherwise Senior. Append the corresponding string to the output. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
